title: Getting random 403 Forbidden errors on the dashboard
body: I keep getting 403 errors when I try to access the TaskFlow dashboard. It's not every time — sometimes it loads fine, sometimes I get a 403 page. It seems totally random. My permissions haven't changed and other people on my team say they're seeing it too. Started happening a couple days ago.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Thanks for reporting this — intermittent 403s are frustrating. To help narrow this down: when you get the 403, is it happening on a specific part of the dashboard (e.g., a particular project, page, or action), or does it happen on the initial dashboard load itself?

A few options that might match what you're seeing:
1. **Any page randomly** — the dashboard sometimes loads, sometimes 403s, no pattern to which page
2. **Specific pages/sections** — certain parts of the dashboard fail while others work fine
3. **After being idle** — it works at first but 403s appear after some time, and a refresh/re-login fixes it
4. **Something else**

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: It's option 1 — any page, totally random. Sometimes I click on the dashboard and it loads fine, other times I get a 403. If I just refresh the page it usually works after a try or two. It doesn't seem tied to any specific section. I thought maybe it was a network issue on my end but my coworkers with the new analyst role are seeing the same thing.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Thanks — that's really helpful. You mentioned your coworkers with **the new analyst role** are seeing the same thing. That jumped out at me:

1. **Are all the affected users on the new analyst role?** Or are people with other roles (e.g., admin, editor) also seeing 403s?
2. **Was this analyst role introduced around the same time the 403s started** (a couple days ago)?

This would help determine whether the issue is role-specific or system-wide.

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: Now that you mention it, yeah — the analyst role was added right around when this started, like two days ago. And I think it is only people with the analyst role having the problem. My manager is an admin and she says everything's been fine for her. I asked a couple of the editors on my team too and they haven't seen any 403s. So it does seem like it's just us analysts. I didn't connect those dots until you pointed it out.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body: [RESOLVED] {"title":"Intermittent 403 Forbidden errors for users with the new analyst role","problem":"Users assigned the recently created 'analyst' role are experiencing random 403 Forbidden errors across all dashboard pages. The errors are intermittent — refreshing usually resolves them temporarily. Users with admin or editor roles are unaffected. The issue started approximately two days ago, coinciding with the introduction of the analyst role.","root_cause_hypothesis":"The analyst role was added recently but its permissions are not being consistently evaluated across requests. Most likely causes: (1) a permissions cache that intermittently serves stale data missing the new role, (2) inconsistent role/permission configuration across multiple app server instances behind a load balancer, or (3) a race condition in permission lookup where the analyst role is not always resolved correctly.","reproduction_steps":["Log in as a user with the analyst role","Navigate to the dashboard","Refresh the page repeatedly — some loads will return 403, others will succeed","Compare with an admin or editor account, which will not experience 403s"],"environment":"Affects all users with the analyst role. Not environment-specific on the client side. Server-side configuration or caching is the likely factor.","severity":"high","impact":"All users with the analyst role are intermittently locked out of the dashboard, blocking their work. The role was presumably created to onboard a group of users who currently have an unreliable experience.","recommended_fix":"1. Check the permission/authorization middleware for how the analyst role is resolved — look for caching layers (Redis, in-memory) that may not have been invalidated when the role was created. 2. If running multiple app instances, verify the role definition and permission grants are consistent across all instances. 3. Check for any role-permission mapping that requires a restart or cache flush to take effect. 4. Review recent deployments or configuration changes from ~2 days ago related to role management.","proposed_test_case":"Create a user with the analyst role and make 50 sequential authenticated requests to the dashboard endpoint. Assert that all return 200. Additionally, add an integration test that creates a new role, assigns permissions, and verifies immediate consistent access without cache-dependent failures.","information_gaps":["Exact deployment topology (load balancer, number of instances) — but the dev team would know this","Whether a permission caching layer exists and its TTL configuration","Whether any deployment or config change was made at the same time as the role creation"]}
